original_datasets.name: not_stated
original_datasets.columns: [DSM5_1..DSM5_23, DSM5_Total, MiniK_1..MiniK_20, MiniK_Total, HKSS_1..HKSS_22, HKSS_Total, Age, Sex, sibling/stepparent indicators]

summary_statistics.info: N=138; Age: mean=34.31, SD=14.74, median=29, min=16, max=69; Sex: 46 male (33%), 92 female (67%). Column types not_stated.

original_datasets.notes: Dataset includes pre-computed totals: DSM5_Total (psychopathology), MiniK_Total and HKSS_Total (life history strategy). Item-level variables are coded DSM5_1..23, MiniK_1..20, HKSS_1..22. Analyses rely on these pre-computed totals as in the proposed plan.

tasks.task_instruction: Conduct the analysis without additional restrictions.

analysis_path.path_description: Correlate DSM5_Total with MiniK_Total and HKSS_Total using non-parametric methods; include sensitivity analyses with covariates and Kendall's tau, with stricter alpha.
preprocessing_steps: Use pre-computed totals: DSM5_Total, MiniK_Total, HKSS_Total.
Optionally examine internal consistency (omega) for DSM5, Mini-K, HKSS (reported in plan but not required for result).
Assess normality (reported violation) and proceed with non-parametric analyses.

key_choices.main_predictor_measure: MiniK_Total and HKSS_Total (slower life history strategy indicated by higher values).
key_choices.sample_restriction: None (N=138). Sensitivity excluding participants <18 years reported in plan.
key_choices.data_processing: Use total scores as provided; coerce Sex to numeric if needed for partial correlations.
key_choices.model_choice: Spearman correlation (primary), Kendall's tau (sensitivity), partial Spearman controlling for Age and Sex (sensitivity).
key_choices.inference_rule: Frequentist p-value with alpha=0.005; Bayesian Kendall’s tau reported in plan but not computed in Python translation.

outcome.name: DSM5_Total
outcome.definition: Sum of DSM-5 Level 1 Cross-Cutting Symptom items (23 items; higher indicates more psychopathology).

main_predictor.name: MiniK_Total and HKSS_Total
main_predictor.definition: Life history strategy totals (higher indicates slower strategy).

controls.name: Sex
controls.definition: Participant sex; used as covariate where available.

analysis_code.notes: Original analyses were conducted in JASP; this is a Python translation using scipy and pingouin for partial Spearman. Replace <dataset.csv> with the original dataset file placed under /app/data.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Follow this planned path; do not change the focal claim, dataset, or analysis goal.

tasks.task_instruction: Report the Mini-K measure instead of HKSS for life history strategy.

analysis_path.path_description: Compute Kendall's tau (and Spearman as supplemental) between DSM5_Total and MiniK_Total with stricter alpha.
preprocessing_steps: Proceed with non-parametric correlation due to non-normality.

key_choices.model_choice: Kendall's tau (primary) with Spearman as secondary.
key_choices.inference_rule: Frequentist p-value with alpha=0.005; Bayesian BF10>10 criterion mentioned in plan but not computed in Python translation.

outcome.name: DSM5_Total
outcome.definition: Sum of DSM-5 Level 1 Cross-Cutting Symptom items.

main_predictor.name: MiniK_Total
main_predictor.definition: Total Mini-K score indicating life history strategy (higher=slower).

analysis_code.notes: Original Task 2 was conducted in JASP. The Python translation reports Kendall's tau and Spearman correlations. Replace <dataset.csv> with the original dataset file placed under /app/data.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Use the Mini-K measure as the sole predictor; do not switch back to HKSS.

files.data/HTJM4_task1__py.py: Python translation of Task 1 non-parametric analyses: computes Spearman and Kendall correlations; optional partial Spearman controlling for Age and Sex when available; writes JSON results.
files.data/HTJM4_task2__py.py: Python translation of Task 2: computes Kendall's tau (primary) and Spearman (supplemental) between DSM5_Total and MiniK_Total; writes JSON results.

codebase.notes: Original analyses were implemented in JASP with .jasp scripts and HTML outputs. For robust, reproducible execution within a Python environment, equivalent scripts were created. Both scripts require the original dataset to be provided as a CSV at /app/data/<dataset.csv> with columns DSM5_Total, MiniK_Total, and HKSS_Total (Task 1), plus Age and Sex for partial correlations if available.

analysis.instructions: Execute Task1 and Task2 using the original dataset placed under /app/data. Produce JSON outputs with correlation statistics for later comparison.
not_allowed_actions: replace the planned path with a different analysis path

debugging_rule.repair_scope: fix dependency, file path, or output extraction issues only; do not redesign the analysis.

analysis.comparison_metrics: Spearman r_s, Kendall tau_b, p-values, partial Spearman coefficients (if available), and qualitative conclusion text regarding support for the claim.
